utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: patient is pamela cook . medical record number is 123546 . she's a 36-year-old female post bilateral reduction mammoplasty on 10-10 20-20 .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: good . how are you ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm doing well . it's good to see you . how have you been ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i've been doing good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: great . how about your breasts , are they doing all right ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: great .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: are you having any chills , fever , nausea , or vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . all right . let's take a peek real quick .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sure .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how's life otherwise ? pretty good ? nothing new ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , just enjoying summertime .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . how's your family ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: they're good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: good . all right . i'm going to take a look at your breast now . if you would just open up your gown for me .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: everything looks good .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how's your back pain ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i'm not really having any more .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any hard spots , lumps , or bumps that you've noticed ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i did when i came in last time when i saw your pa , ruth sanchez in march . she said i , she said she found a lump right here under my left breast , but i have n't felt it since then . but i did the massages .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , well . that that's good . uh , it's probably just the scar tissue , but everything looks good and you're healing wonderful , so .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i told her that the scars here was kind of bothering me and i got scar gel . i was using it everyday , but i do n't think i need it now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: yeah , that scar did widen a little bit . let me take a closer look , hang on . this one widened a little too , ? the incisions are well healed though with no signs of infection or any redness on either breast , so i'm not concerned .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but this one just bothered me a little bit more .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i understand . um , you can close your gown now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: the only thing that is really going to help out that is to uh , to cut it out and re-close it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and you do n't want that , ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i mean , not right now .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , you want to come back and revisit um , maybe six months ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , i will do that . i still have n't , i still have some more of the gel and i can try using that again .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . keep doing that twice a day . the gel is going to lighten the color a little bit , which is already pretty light . um , but , just in that area , and it's high tension , so it's going to rub a little bit .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , but it kind of bothers me a little bit .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: uh , i do see that . like i said , the only way to really fix that is to cut it out .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh- .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , let's take a look in six months and then we'll go from there . sound like a plan ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but we have n't hit a full year yet .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i know . um , i would n't do any revisions anyway for scar tissue until we're at least a year out anyway .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so let's wait those six months . you can keep using uh , the mederma scar gel twice a day . massage and scar gel will help for the scars . um , you can put it on other scars too , if you need .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , so that's what i would do . let's just get some pictures today so we can keep up um , with them . and keep an eye on these scars and then we'll go from there .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well it's good to see you . i'm glad you're doing well .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , same here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . well , i'm going to tell the front desk six months and we'll revisit those scars .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: thank you . they're gon na come get your photos now , okay ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 36-year-old female who is status post bilateral reduction mammoplasty on 10/10/2020.
Breast
The doctor asks if the patient has any fever, chills, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient denies fever, chills, nausea, or vomiting.
The doctor performs a physical examination of the breasts.
The doctor notes that the breasts look good.
The doctor asks about the patient's back pain.
The patient reports that her back pain has resolved.
The doctor asks if the patient has noticed any hard spots, lumps, or bumps.
The patient notes a previously identified lump under the left breast has resolved with massage.
The doctor confirms the lump was likely scar tissue and notes the patient is healing well.
The doctor observes bilateral widening of the scar tissue but notes incisions are well healed with no signs of infection or erythema.
The doctor explains that surgical revision is the only way to address the widened scar tissue.
The doctor asks if the patient wants to pursue surgical revision.
The patient declines surgical revision at this time.
The doctor asks if the patient wants to return for a follow-up in six months.
The patient agrees to a six-month follow-up and plans to resume using scar gel.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue using scar gel twice daily.
The doctor plans a follow-up visit in six months.
The doctor instructs the patient to continue using Mederma scar gel and perform scar massage twice daily.
The doctor orders bilateral breast photos to monitor scarring.

case_description: follow up on bilateral reduction mammoplasty; scar tissue
